content: Around 200 bats have died, and prima facie it appears that they succumbed to heatstroke, says Katghora Divisional Forest Officer Kumar Nishant

title: Rising prices in Tamil Nadu and a ship-sized hole in household budget
content: Fuel costs have risen steeply, thrice in the span of 10 days, as the Iran war continues to choke supplies globally. This has also led to an increase in the prices across the board, with grocery, food, and commute rates burgeoning. Many people in Tamil Nadu find the going very difficult

title: Hutti dazzles as gold prices soar in world market
content: Hutti Gold Mines, the only functional gold mine in the country, earn a whopping ₹633.34 crores more in 2025-26, thanks to skyrocketing price of gold in world market

title: Centre cracked down on CJP after IB warning on possible unrest, claim Maharashtra Congress leaders
content: Former State Congress chiefs Nana Patole and Balasaheb Thorat alleged that the government was suppressing people's anger instead of addressing issues faced by the youth, poor and unemployed

title: Degree student among four booked for dangerous late-night car racing in Hyderabad’s Knowledge City
content: According to the police, the car was being driven in a rash and dangerous manner, making heavy sounds and repeatedly taking rounds

content: (CBI). They allegedly told her that her bank accounts were linked to a money laundering case and warned her not to disclose the matter to anyone, claiming she was under “digital arrest.”

content: Sambit Patra claimed Mr. Gandhi's "plan" was exposed after Congress leader reportedly said at a meeting of the advisory council of the Congress' Minority Department that the Modi Government will fall within a year

title: Revaluation process a hassle for Class 12 CBSE students in Chennai
content: Students have taken to social media to air their grievances with many noting the blurry images of their scanned copies, the half marks awarded to MCQ questions and the notification of blank pages on a written answer sheet

title: Bengaluru school headmaster arrested under Pocso Act, after minor student found pregnant
content: The accused, identified as Chandraiah, has been serving as the headmaster of a government school in Srirampura. According to police, the alleged abuse had been taking place over the past year

content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio emphasises that his visit is not focused on “restoring” ties, but rather on enhancing what he described as a “very solid and strong strategic partnership”

title: Delhi court orders framing charges against two CBI officials in corruption case
content: Delhi court orders corruption charges against two CBI officials for compromising investigations, while discharging others due to insufficient evidence

title: 17 officers from three services graduate from Air Force Test Pilots School
content: This year, the graduating course comprised 17 officers including 14 officers from the Indian Air Force (IAF), one officer from Indian Army and two officers from Indian Navy

title: 12 MPs, four Parliamentary panels selected for Sansad Ratna Awards
content: In a statement, the Prime Point Foundation said BJP's Jagdambika Pal (Uttar Pradesh), P.P. Chaudhary (Rajasthan), Nishikant Dubey (Jharkhand) and Shiv Sena's Shrikant Eknath Shinde (Maharashtra) are among the MPs chosen in the individual category

title: AIADMK leader Edappadi Palaniswami criticises unscheduled power cuts across Tamil Nadu
content: Mr. Palaniswami, in a statement, contended normal life was affected due to unscheduled power cuts and people had to spend sleepless nights.

title: Vizianagaram wears deserted look in heatwave; Collector advises staying indoors
content: Day temperatures touch 42°C in Vizianagaram, the Ring Road junction lies near-empty and many shops and hotels report poor business, residents staying home through the afternoon

title: Tiger kills woman in M.P.'s Bandhavgarh reserve area; angry locals ‘assault’ forest staff
content: Villagers accused forest officials of failing to respond promptly despite repeated calls for help and claimed the tiger was still roaming nearby

title: Former MP P. Venugopal quits AIADMK
content: Mr. Venugopal said the party’s general secretary (Edappadi K. Palaniswami) should use better judgment. “A person who is a Rajya Sabha MP is given a ticket to contest (Assembly polls) without thinking what will happen if he wins the MLA seat.”

title: What did the Supreme Court say about bail under UAPA?
content: What are the disagreements between recent Supreme Court rulings on UAPA bail? Why is bail so difficult under Section 43D(5) of the UAPA? What did the Court say in K.A. Najeeb about prolonged incarceration and bail? What role does Article 21 play in UAPA bail jurisprudence?

content: The U.S. Secretary of State is in India for a four-day visit to hold bilateral talks, and attend the Quad Foreign Ministers’ Meeting

title: Punjab Police ASI found dead in Amritsar
content: A police team, led by the Station House Officer of Majithia police station, reached the spot soon after the information was received. A forensic team also examined the scene, says SSP

title: West Bengal elections 2026: Who lost the most?
content: Wiped out from the North, South-west and Greater Kolkata regions in West Bengal, Trinamool has been reduced to 80 seats – its lowest since 2011

content: The repolling in Falta was conducted amid massive security deployment with armoured cars, drone surveillance and hundreds of Central forces on the ground

title: ITBP officials meet Kanpur top cop seeking action in medical negligence case of jawan’s mother
content: The ITBP personnel accused doctors at a private hospital of negligence that allegedly led to the amputation of his mother’s right hand; he demanded a fair investigation and strict action against the hospital administration and the doctors involved.

title: 3,000 women committees formed, 81 houses demolished in anti-drug campaign in J&K: Manoj Sinha
content: The Lieutenant Governor said while 59 traffickers have been arrested under PIT‑NDPS provisions, 81 narcotic palaces built with the traffickers’ black money have been demolished

title: Government in 'advanced stage of consultation' on industry's demand for cotton import duty removal: Official
content: A delegation comprising apparel industry representatives and exporters met Vice President C.P. Radhakrishnan and several Union Ministers recently and sought the removal of the duty

title: India issues advisory against non-essential travel to Congo, Uganda and South Sudan amid Ebola outbreak
content: As of May 23, the total reported death toll from the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola has risen to 216. The total number of cases, including suspected and confirmed, stands at 968

title: Falta constituency results 2026: Why are the votes for the West Bengal seat being counted now?
content: Voting for the Falta Assembly constituency seat was originally held on April 29 2026, as part of the second phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections.

title: Central University of Rajasthan launches research promotion scheme for scholars
content: Under the scheme, the research scholars will receive an additional financial support of ₹5,000 per month over and above the existing non-NET fellowship of the UGC. An additional contingency grant of ₹10,000 will also be provided to support research-related expenses

title: U.P. pushes to strengthen sewerage system, prevent river pollution with multi-pronged approach
content: “Untreated sewage from U.P. cities will no longer flow directly into rivers. Under the Namami Gange Mission Phase-2, sewerage infrastructure is being significantly strengthened,” Additional Chief Secretary, Namami Ganga and Rural Drinking Water Supply Department, UP

content: Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren and his wife Kalpana Soren had to return after waiting for hours to campaign in Jhargram as they were not allowed to land, the party says

title: Security stepped up at Jhalmuri stall which PM visited during WB election campaign
content: Bikram Kumar Sau, the Jhalmuri vendor, lodged a written complaint at the Jhargram police station, alleging that he had been receiving threatening phone calls and messages from people threatening to kill him.

title: Kasaragod district administration prepares railway master plan to end travel hardships
content: District Collector Arjun Pandian has formally written to railway authorities seeking the extension of major train services terminating at Kannur and Mangaluru to Kasaragod, restoration of discontinued passenger services, and stoppages for key superfast trains at Kasaragod railway station

title: Manipur CM assures NIA probe into abduction of Naga men
content: Kuki Inpi Manipur extends shutdown since May 13 by another 48 hours to secure release of 14 Kuki people

title: A nation that makes its own weapons writes its destiny: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said future wars would increasingly be determined by advancements in munitions, automation, and emerging technologies rather than merely the size of armed forces

title: Supreme Court registers suo motu case over death of Twisha Sharma
content: A three-judge Bench comprising Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M. Pancholi is scheduled to hear the matter on Monday (May 25)

title: Surya Kiran Aerobatic Team to celebrate 30th anniversary on May 26 at Bidar
content: Since 2015, the team has been operating the Hawk Mk-132 aircraft in its signature red-and-white colour scheme and nine-aircraft formation

title: News in Frames | The drying naulas of Uttarakhand
content: Many of the naulas and dharas, the water springs which represent a union of nature and human ingenuity, are drying up in signs of a deepening environmental crisis across Uttarakhand

title: BJP President Nitin Nabin’s Birthday: Melody chocolates distributed among visually impaired children in Patna
content: Mr. Nabin was in Patna on May 23, 2026, and welcomed a few party leaders at his residence who had come to wish him on his 46th birthday

title: Surviving a city on ₹13,690
content: The Uttar Pradesh government increased its minimum wage after 12 years by ₹2,377 last month. While workers are marginally relieved, those in Noida’s SEZ say the amount still barely sustains them, and there is little left to save. Shrimansi Kaushik finds that workers’ spaces and aspirations have shrunk over time

content: The allegation of corruption in government jobs came to hurt the Trinamool Congress and the party lost the recently concluded assembly elections to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

content: Kotwali Assistant Commissioner of Police said that in the incident, which occurred in the early hours of the day in the Namo Ghat area under the Adampur police station limits, four persons have been detained.

content: A 39yearold man has died after being attacked by a shark at the Great Barrier Reef on Sunday, police said, the second fatal shark attack in Australia this month.  The

content: The weather will be an important factor in determining where a plume of chemicals would go in the event of an explosion.

content: Trump had earlier said he might not attend the wedding due to tensions involving Iran and ongoing presidential responsibilities.

content: Marco Rubio Press Conference With S Jaishankar On Iran War: "Possibility That World Will Get Some Good News In Next Few Hours"

content: The glaring findings are part of a voluminous 7,500-page chargesheet filed by the NIA on May 14 in connection with the high-intensity vehicle-borne IED blast that rocked the national capital on November 10 last year.

content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

content: Iranian officials have stressed that gaps between the sides persist and the dispute over the nuclear program would not be part of initial negotiations.

content: Several BJP leaders claimed that Rahul Gandhi's "plan" was exposed after he reportedly said that the Modi government would fall within a year.

content: In a post on X, the agency said, "ICE Los Angeles arrested Parminderpal Singh, 26, of India, on May 21. He is in ICE custody pending removal."

title: Life Convict Walked Out Of Bengaluru Jail Using Fake Court Order 8 Years Ago
content: The prison authorities have now filed a case against the convict and others involved in the fabrication of fake Supreme Court documents.

title: Twisha Sharma Death Case Updates: Twisha Sharma's Funeral To Be Held Today Evening In Bhopal
content: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: The case came under scrutiny as the Madhya Pradesh Police faced criticism for an inefficient probe into the case, while Giribala Singh was accused of exerting undue influence.

title: US Envoy Sergio Gor's India-Inspired Look Steals Spotlight In Delhi
content: Gor was seen dressed in a classic black tuxedo featuring striking gold embroidery on the shoulders. His outfit added a regal touch to the formal diplomatic evening.

title: Violence Inside Kapurthala Central Jail Triggers Political Row In Punjab
content: The disturbance reportedly erupted on Saturday night after an argument involving inmates escalated into a violent clash inside the jail premises.

title: BJP Ahead In Falta Where Repoll Was Held Over Alleged Voting Fraud
content: Repolling was ordered in all 285 booths in Falta after allegations of tampering with the voting machines and electoral irregularities during April 29 voting.

title: "US, India Not Just Allies, We're Strategic Allies": Rubio To S Jaishankar
content: Marco Rubio, who began his four-day India visit yesterday, met his Indian counterpart S Jaishankar this morning.

title: Twisha Sharma's Husband Recalls Dispute Over Ajmer Trip Hours Before Her Death
content: Messages exchanged between Twisha Sharma and her mother had mentioned tensions over her pregnancy, while detailing a hostile environment at her in-laws' home.

title: 24 killed as explosives-laden car rams into train carrying Pakistan military personnel
content: At least 24 Pakistani army personnel were killed and more than 100 injured after a suicide vehicle bomb targeted a military shuttle train in Quetta, Balochistan, on Sunday. The blast derailed a bogie, sparked a fire and damaged nearby homes and vehicles. The banned Baloch Liberation Army’s Majeed Brigade claimed responsibility, saying army personnel were the intended target.

title: From anti India racism to visa issues - Key takeaways from Rubio-Jaishankar joint press conference
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and external affairs minister S Jaishankar reaffirmed strong US-India strategic ties, highlighting cooperation in defence, trade, energy and technology. Rubio said US visa reforms were global, not India-specific, while condemning racist remarks against Indian Americans. Both leaders discussed energy security, Quad cooperation, trade negotiations, Iran tensions and India’s multi-alignment foreign policy approach.

title: After 12 days, Twisha Sharma’s last rites to be held in Bhopal today at 5pm
content: Twisha Sharma's final rites are set for 5 PM in Bhopal, a decision made due to heat and medical concerns, her cousin stated.  A second post-mortem is underway by AIIMS doctors following a High Court order.  Her husband, Samarth Singh, arrested after absconding, is in police custody.  The Supreme Court will hear the case Monday.

title: 'Thank the court': BJP leader welcomes HC relief for Vinesh Phogat in WFI dispute
content: The Delhi high court’s order came after the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) had declared Vinesh ineligible for domestic events till June 26. Babita, herself a former wrestler, said the court’s decision was correct.

title: Cockroach Janata Party founder’s house in Maharashtra gets police protection
content: Police protection has been deployed outside the residence of Abhijeet Dipke in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar after the online rise of the Cockroach Janata Party. Police said the move was precautionary due to growing public attention, not threats. The group recently gained popularity through memes and commentary on unemployment, exam paper leaks and education issues. Dipke also alleged its social media accounts and website were compromised.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: 'We will not stop': Rahul vows more NEET protests until education minister resigns
content: Rahul Gandhi demands Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG 2026 controversy, vowing continued protests until a foolproof system prevents future paper leaks. The NSUI also called for the minister's resignation and an impartial investigation, as the NTA claims only some questions, not the full paper, were leaked.

title: 30-year membership wait, ‘green-card’ system: Inside Delhi's elite Gymkhana Club
content: The Centre has ordered the historic Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its 27.3-acre Lutyens' Delhi premises by June 5, citing urgent need for defence infrastructure and public security. This move, stemming from governance disputes and alleged mismanagement, has shocked members and sparked debate over elite colonial clubs on prime government land.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Iran hits back at Rubio’s ‘energy hostage’ remark, blames US sanctions for market turmoil
content: Iran has rejected US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's remarks, accusing Washington of distorting regional realities and diverting attention from its destabilizing policies. Tehran asserts that US sanctions, not its own actions, have disrupted global energy markets and held them hostage. Rubio had previously accused Iran of holding the energy market hostage during his visit to India.

title: Palo Networks CEO: It’s mistake to believe AI productivity gains means fewer employees
content: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora refutes the notion that AI will lead to job cuts, calling it a 'fallacy'. He argues that AI's efficiency gains will instead fuel long-delayed projects and business transformations, requiring more, not fewer, employees. Arora believes AI will help clear backlogs and create capacity for new hires with evolving skill sets.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 67: Can Ranveer Singh film net Rs 1150 crore this weekend?
content: Aditya Dhar's historic spy thriller 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', starring Ranveer Singh, enters its final theatrical stretch on Day 67. With an official JioHotstar domestic OTT release date locked for June 4, 2026, the box office juggernaut is racing to secure its next massive milestone. The film currently sits just Rs 3.65 crore away from crossing the monumental Rs 1150 crore net domestic mark in India. Fans looking forward to the high-octane action can also gear up for its dual-platform digital release next month, which features the unfiltered "Raw & Undekha" cut.

title: Canadian GP: Russell snatches pole as Antonelli, McLaren and Ferrari up for fierce battle
content: George Russell clinched pole for the Canadian Grand Prix, just after winning the Sprint race. His Mercedes teammate, championship leader Andrea Kimi Antonelli, starts second. McLaren and Ferrari are pushing hard, while Red Bull faces challenges. An intense intra-team rivalry between Russell and Antonelli adds further drama to the highly anticipated race.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is not giving up on China and the reason is next-gen CPUs
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang confirmed China remains crucial for the company's $1 trillion sales forecast, including a new $200 billion CPU market. Despite US-China tensions, Huang emphasized serving the vital Chinese market. Nvidia is also ramping up production for its next-generation 'Vera' CPU and GPU platform, aiming to capture the burgeoning agentic AI sector.

title: 'That is the bottom line': Rohit Sharma reveals big India dream
content: Rohit Sharma expressed his ambition for India to become the undisputed powerhouse of world cricket, citing recent successes across formats and age groups as evidence of strong progress. He also noted the game's evolution, particularly in white-ball cricket, with a more fearless and aggressive batting approach now setting new standards.

title: China’s 'super carrier' push: World's largest naval support ship under construction
content: China is reportedly nearing the launch of its largest naval support vessel, a massive ship designed to bolster its growing blue-water capabilities. Images reveal significant construction progress, suggesting a potential completion by year-end and service entry between 2027-2028. This development underscores China's expanding naval ambitions.

title: UP woman ends life after video call with husband; family alleges harassment
content: A 25-year-old woman allegedly died by suicide after an argument with her husband over a video call. Her family claims she was harassed by her husband and in-laws, leading to her returning to her parental home. Police are investigating the allegations of harassment and abetment to suicide.

title: India’s fuel demand growth may slow sharply in H2 2026 amid price hikes, austerity push
content: India's transportation fuel demand growth is set for a sharp slowdown in late 2026. Higher prices, government conservation efforts, and a weakening rupee are impacting mobility. Analysts have significantly cut growth forecasts, with petrol demand facing the steepest decline due to reduced commuting and discretionary travel.

title: Tourists stranded for hours as weekend rush triggers Manali traffic nightmare
content: Manali experienced severe traffic congestion late Saturday night due to a surge in tourist arrivals. Long vehicle queues formed across the hill town, leaving many commuters and tourists stranded for hours. The slow traffic movement, attributed to the weekend rush, caused significant inconvenience.

title: What is Trump's 'America First' visa policy? Here's what it means for Indians
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced a new 'America First' visa scheduling system in India, prioritizing business professionals and investors crucial to US economic and strategic interests. This initiative aims to strengthen bilateral ties and expedite travel for those contributing to trade and investment, while also emphasizing stricter security screenings for all applicants.

title: Hyderabad lawyer fighting waqf property cases dies after Scorpio rams him outside home; watch
content: An advocate fighting waqf property encroachment cases was fatally run over by an SUV in Hyderabad. His son alleges two city educationists are behind the murder, citing previous threats and attacks. Police have formed special teams to investigate the incident.

title: Market recap: 6 of top-10 most-valued firms add over Rs 74,000 crore
content: India's top companies saw a significant market valuation surge last week, with Reliance Industries leading the gains. Despite a volatile trading period, the BSE Sensex managed a modest advance. While several giants added billions, others like Bharti Airtel and Hindustan Unilever experienced notable dips. Analysts point to global optimism and domestic buying as drivers for the market's recovery.

title: 'Obsession with our country's most cherished structure': Trump reacts to White House shooting
content: US President Donald Trump praised the Secret Service and law enforcement for their swift action in neutralizing a gunman near the White House. The suspect, identified as Nasire Best, was killed after firing at agents. Trump highlighted the incident's importance for future presidential security, referencing a previous security breach.

title: Rishabh Pant to be sacked as captain? LSG reveal plans after IPL 2026
content: Lucknow Super Giants are poised for a significant shake-up after a dismal IPL 2026 season. Global director Tom Moody acknowledged the team's failure to meet expectations, citing leadership challenges and hinting at major changes. Rishabh Pant's captaincy and batting form have come under scrutiny, with the franchise considering a complete reset to regain its competitive edge.

title: Divyenndu On Telugu Debut: Actor mugs up long lines for Peddi; says he fell in love again
content: Mirzapur's Divyenndu Sharma makes a thrilling Telugu debut in 'Peddi' as the menacing Rambujji, alongside Ram Charan. He rediscovered his passion for filmmaking while learning a new language and culture for the period sports action drama. The film, featuring Ram Charan in multiple sports, is set for a June 4 release.

title: Shreyas Iyer joins Kohli, Samson, becomes 4th captain in IPL to...
content: Shreyas Iyer's maiden IPL century, an unbeaten 101, powered Punjab Kings to a crucial seven-wicket win against Lucknow Super Giants. This stellar knock, featuring a 140-run partnership, kept PBKS's playoff hopes alive and marked a historic achievement for Iyer as a captain. The victory ended a six-match losing streak for Punjab.

title: ‘They will bring justice’: Twisha’s father hopeful as AIIMS team reaches Bhopal for 2nd autopsy
content: Following a directive from the Madhya Pradesh High Court, an AIIMS medical team has arrived in Bhopal to conduct a second post-mortem on Twisha Sharma. Her father, buoyed by the rule of law, is optimistic that the investigation will yield justice and curb the public shaming of his daughter even after her death.

title: Ebola outbreak: India issues travel advisory against three African countries
content: In light of the ongoing Ebola crisis, India has advised its citizens to reconsider any unnecessary travel plans to the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, and South Sudan. The World Health Organization has issued alarms regarding the rapid transmission of the virus, with Uganda documenting new cases of the Bundibugyo strain and a concerning rise in fatalities in Congo.

title: Iran refutes Trump's 'largely negotiated' deal, claims to keep Hormuz control
content: President Donald Trump announced a significant deal with Iran is nearing completion. This agreement aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end ongoing hostilities. While Iran has pushed back on some claims, discussions are ongoing. Regional leaders have expressed support for the peace efforts. The proposed deal also addresses Iran's nuclear program.

title: Another fuel price hike coming? BPCL says rise ‘inevitable’ if crisis continues
content: Fuel retailers face mounting losses as global energy disruptions persist, potentially forcing further petrol and diesel price hikes. BPCL director Raj Kumar Dubey indicated limited options for policymakers, including price increases or government funding. India is diversifying oil sources and accelerating its push towards green energy to mitigate these challenges.

title: ‘Jumped from 1st floor to escape’: 2 arrested in Coimbatore child rape-murder case
content: A 10-year-old girl was found sexually assaulted and murdered in Coimbatore, leading to widespread outrage. Police swiftly apprehended her neighbour, K Karthi, and an accomplice within 24 hours. Karthi, who sustained injuries while attempting to evade arrest, confessed to the heinous crime. The Chief Minister has pledged stringent punishment and enhanced safety measures for women and children.

title: From Hormuz to nuclear talks - what to expect in the 60-day US-Iran ceasefire proposal
content: A potential 60-day ceasefire extension between the US and Iran is nearing, aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and allow Iranian oil exports. This deal, brokered by Pakistan, includes lifting US port blockades and sanctions waivers, contingent on Iran negotiating its nuclear program and ending the Israel-Hezbollah conflict. Global oil markets are closely monitoring developments.

title: Clothes on fire, rooftop protests and tear gas: Chaos erupts inside Punjab jail
content: Violence and arson erupted in Kapurthala Central Jail late Saturday night following a minor altercation between inmates. Unverified videos show inmates on rooftops claiming they were thrashed and shot at, with one reportedly suffering a bullet injury. Authorities stated tear gas was used and the situation was under control.

title: 'Well done, Arjun': Sachin pens emotional note after son’s IPL 2026 debut
content: Arjun Tendulkar impressed on his IPL debut for Lucknow Super Giants with figures of 4-0-36-1 against Punjab Kings, including the wicket of Prabhsimran Singh. Proud father Sachin Tendulkar shared a rare emotional message, praising Arjun’s patience, hard work, and attitude after waiting all season for an opportunity in IPL 2026.

title: White House shooter Nasire Best, 21, who identified himself as Jesus Christ, shot dead
content: A 21-year-old man, Nasire Best, who allegedly believed he was Jesus Christ, was killed by Secret Service after opening fire outside the White House. Best had a history of loitering and violating orders to stay away from the complex. The incident triggered a lockdown and injured at least one bystander.

title: Imtiaz reacts to clash between 'Main Vaapas Aaunga' and Kangana’s 'Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata'
content: Bollywood's 90s superstar Govinda recently shared a dramatic revelation on 'The Kapil Sharma Show'.  He detailed how a relentless work schedule led to hospitalization, prompting a life-saving intervention from the legendary Dilip Kumar.  Kumar's stark advice to abandon 25 films, coupled with a practical financial solution, ultimately saved Govinda's health and career.

title: Trinamool councillor Sanjay Das found hanging at Kolkata home
content: Tragedy struck within Kolkata's political landscape as Sanjay Das, a councillor from the Trinamool party, was discovered dead at his residence. Early indications point towards suicide, with family members and locals attributing his distress to mounting pressures from land-grab and extortion allegations.

title: H-1B to ‘maybe’: Green card dream runs into bureaucratic nightmare
content: a sweeping new directive issued by the US Citizenship and Immigration Services . most temporary visa holders seeking permanent residency will now be required to leave the US and apply for green cards from their home countries . the policy marks one of the most dramatic attempts yet by the Trump administration to curb illegal immigration .

title: 'Lured foreigners with fat perks': Govt says 217 Indians joined Russian army, 49 dead
content: as many as 217 Indians have joined Russian forces, of which 49 died and six are missing in action in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war . the govt said the Indian Embassy is making efforts to bring back the 139 people who have been released from military service .
